topic: Polynomial-delay exact vertex calculus for sharp ATE bounds in the discrete IV model with MULTI-VALUED instruments (binary treatment D, outcome Y in [n], instrument Z in [l], no monotonicity), closing the l>2 gap that Boushehrian-Akbari et al. (arXiv:2604.12802) explicitly defer and the projection Song-Guo-Chan-Richardson (arXiv:2405.09510) explicitly defer: work over the GAUGE-FIXED SECTION H0(n,l) = H(n,l) cap {lambda_(0,0,z)=0, z=2..l} of the sharp-ATE dual polyhedron H(n,l)={lambda : (A'lambda)_tau >= c_tau for all canonical types tau=(delta,eta)}, since H itself has lineality space {per-arm-block constants summing to zero} of dimension l-1 and hence no vertices, while H0 is pointed and meets each gauge orbit once so its vertices biject with the M-classes (lambda ~ lambda' iff A'lambda = A'lambda'); conjecture (I-a) the ACTIVITY-SET RECOGNITION problem VERT-REC -- given (n,l) in unary and an explicit type list B, decide whether some vertex lambda of H0(n,l) has Sig(lambda) := {tau : (A'lambda)_tau = c_tau} equal to B -- is solvable in time polynomial in n+l+|B|, which is non-trivial because the defining system has 2^l*n^2 inequalities so the naive test is exponential in l; conjecture (ii), THE CENTRAL THEOREM, that there is a POLYNOMIAL-DELAY SOUND-AND-COMPLETE enumerator of the vertices of H0(n,l) (input (n,l) in unary, each output the rational coefficient vector in R^(2nl), delay polynomial in n+l), so the sharp bounds become L(P)=max and U(P)=min of explicit linear functionals of P(y,d|z) in analytical closed form instead of a numerically solved LP -- non-tautological since vertex enumeration for inequality-presented polyhedra admits no polynomial-delay algorithm unless P=NP (Khachiyan-Boros-Borys-Elbassioni-Gurvich, DCG 2008); and conjecture (iii) STRICT INCOMPLETENESS BY CARDINALITY of the arm-indexed l>2 family of arXiv:2604.12802, whose size is at most l*((l-1)^(n-1)-(l-1)) (at most l*((l-1)^n-(l-1)) under the alternative reading of its exponent). NO counting-formula theorem is claimed: N(n,l) is just the enumerator's output size, with N(n,2)=5*4^(n-1)-2^(n+2)+4 retained only as a consistency check against the published l=2 result. The recognition algorithm, the enumerator, and the deficit are the solve's derivation work. Exact rational witness at (n,l)=(2,3): a THREE-ARM-ESSENTIAL vertex of H0 with lambda blocks (2,1,2,1),(0,-2,-2,0),(0,1,0,1) in the order ((y=0,d=0),(y=1,d=0),(y=0,d=1),(y=1,d=1)), verified over Q by nine independent checks -- zero of 32 dual constraints violated, section conditions satisfied, q* normalized and nonnegative with Aq*=p exactly, strong duality c'q*=lambda'p=101/2004, |Sig|=12 with rank 12-of-12 vertex certification, and all three arm-blocks non-constant so it cannot arise from any two-arm sub-instrument; the same exact pipeline reproduces the published l=2 count exactly at (2,2) (8 of 8) and certifies at least 38 M-classes at (2,3) versus A1's at-most-6. Estimation rung: the endpoints are a max/min of finitely many explicit linear functionals of the cell probabilities over an index set the enumerator supplies in closed form, so plug-in estimation uses empirical cell frequencies and inference proceeds by intersection-bounds / max-of-means over a FINITE EXPLICIT index set -- exactly what current numerical-LP practice lacks. Consumers: judge/examiner-IV designs (Dobbie-Goldin-Yang AER 2018; Frandsen-Lefgren-Leslie Ecta 2023; Kling AER 2006), which collapse a many-armed judge instrument to a scalar leniency index and would instead report an assumption-free sharp ATE interval using all arms; and causaloptim's optimize_effect_2, whose generic vertex enumeration is its documented bottleneck

open_problems.open_problem: Produce a complete irredundant affine-facet catalogue and constructive boundary-distribution certificate for every sharp ATE endpoint face in the binary-IV model with simultaneous defier and never-taker caps.

source_refs.excerpt: does not yet fix the final count of affine facets; Stage 0 must compute and name the catalogue
need either an honest exposed-basis enumeration

open_problems.why_unsolved: The scalar envelope reduction was judged correct, but generic LP duality did not establish that the proposed five facet families were exhaustive or nonredundant. Complementary slackness also failed to supply the promised facet-by-facet primal maps, especially on degenerate zero-cell and simultaneous cap-binding faces.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An irredundant max/min-of-affine endpoint formula with a proved exhaustive facet count and an explicit endpoint-attaining latent distribution for every facet and boundary regime.

writing_exemplar.why: Its IV-bound workflow explains why explicit active forms matter for both interpretation and inference.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: IV assumptions yield partial identification -> sharp endpoints are optimized functionals -> numerical solutions hide the active identifying inequality -> enumerate and certify every endpoint face

method_exemplars.technique: Symbolically eliminate finite response-type masses and pair every exposed dual inequality with an endpoint-attaining primal distribution.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The unresolved object is an exhaustive analytic representation of a finite sharp-IV polytope.

method_exemplars.technique: Derive the directional derivative of finite max/min affine maps and use derivative-aware resampling at ties.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Once the facet list is explicit, the endpoint estimator is a nonsmooth finite maximum or minimum of empirical affine forms.

open_problems.open_problem: Does the gauge-fixed sharp-ATE dual for binary treatment, n-valued outcome, and l>2 instrument admit a sound-and-complete polynomial-delay enumeration of all M-distinct vertices from the implicit latent-type system?

source_refs.claim: The paper characterizes sharp ATE bounds completely only for a binary instrument, gives only a lower-bound family for multivalued instruments, and defers sharp multivalued characterizations.
source_refs.author: Arefe Boushehrian, Mohammad Reza Badri, Sina Akbari, Negar Kiyavash

source_refs.claim: General vertex generation for inequality-presented unbounded polyhedra has no total-polynomial algorithm unless P=NP, while the bounded-polytope case remains open.
source_refs.author: Leonid Khachiyan, Endre Boros, Konrad Borys, Khaled Elbassioni, Vladimir Gurvich

open_problems.why_unsolved: The multivalued dual has 2^l n^2 latent-response-type inequalities and is degenerate before quotienting by its l-1 dimensional lineality space. The binary-instrument signature bijection uses two-arm structural identities that do not directly extend to three-arm-essential vertices, while generic polyhedral enumeration is complexity-theoretically hard. A positive result must expose IV-matrix structure that avoids scanning the implicit exponential inequality family.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A polynomial-delay rational-output enumerator for all vertices of a canonical pointed section, with soundness, completeness, and bit-complexity proofs.

writing_exemplar.why: It moves directly from numerical LP bounds to analytical vertex formulas and ends by naming the multivalued characterization gap.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: sharp discrete-IV ATE bounds are LP optima -> analytical formulas are indexed by dual vertices -> binary-instrument vertices have an explicit signature calculus -> obtain a complete output-sensitive calculus for l>2

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt the binary active-constraint rank lemmas, admissible-signature encoding, and signature-to-vertex reconstruction proof.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It solves the identical dual-vertex-to-sharp-bound problem for l=2.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt reverse search by defining a parent map and proving connectivity and polynomial-time local adjacency generation on the IV-specific vertex graph.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Polynomial delay would follow from an IV-specific reverse-search graph whose nodes are exactly the desired dual vertices.

method_exemplars.technique: Port bias correction, Gaussian approximation, and adaptive inequality selection for extrema of estimated linear bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Once enumerated, each endpoint is a finite maximum or minimum of empirical linear functionals.

open_problems.open_problem: Is exact recognition of a proposed active latent-type set as the signature of some multivalued-IV sharp-ATE dual vertex polynomial-time in n+l plus the explicit signature size?

source_refs.claim: For l=2 the paper proves a bijection between admissible signatures and M-distinct vertices, but for l>2 supplies only an explicit subfamily and defers the full characterization.
source_refs.author: Arefe Boushehrian, Mohammad Reza Badri, Sina Akbari, Negar Kiyavash

source_refs.claim: Vertex enumeration becomes total-polynomial under bounded branch-width, illustrating that tractability requires exploitable structure beyond a generic inequality description.
source_refs.author: Arne C. Reimers, Leen Stougie

open_problems.why_unsolved: A direct test must verify tightness against an implicit family of 2^l n^2 constraints and establish full rank modulo gauge directions. The l=2 admissibility rules compress these checks into local binary conditions, but no analogous multiarity certificate is known; generic face recognition does not automatically exploit the product structure of canonical IV response types.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Either a polynomial-time recognition algorithm with a succinct certificate and rank proof, or a hardness theorem identifying the parameter regime where recognition is impossible.

writing_exemplar.why: Its admissible-signature narrative naturally formulates recognition as the missing local primitive behind enumeration.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: dual vertices index sharp formulas -> binary vertices are recognized through admissible signatures -> multivalued latent types make naive checking exponential -> characterize realizable signatures in polynomial time

method_exemplars.technique: Generalize the binary signature rank decomposition and reconstruction lemmas into a succinct multivalued certificate.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The binary theorem is an exact activity-set recognition result for the same dual.

method_exemplars.technique: Use locally checkable bases and pivot adjacency as certificates connecting recognition to reverse-search enumeration.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Recognition of valid nodes and local neighbors is precisely the oracle needed for polynomial-delay reverse search.

open_problems.open_problem: For l>2, is the arm-indexed vertex family constructed by Boushehrian et al. strictly incomplete, in the sense that a genuinely multi-arm M-class of sharp-ATE dual vertices exists outside that family?

source_refs.claim: The multivalued-instrument result constructs an explicit M-distinct family only to prove an exponential lower bound; it does not claim exhaustiveness and leaves the sharp characterization open.
source_refs.author: Arefe Boushehrian, Mohammad Reza Badri, Sina Akbari, Negar Kiyavash

source_refs.claim: The categorical-IV analysis finds that retaining all instrument arms can strictly tighten plug-in identification bounds relative to deleting an arm.
source_refs.author: Yilin Song, F. Richard Guo, K. C. Gary Chan, Thomas S. Richardson

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published l>2 construction varies a distinguished arm and a restricted combinatorial pattern, which proves a lower bound but not completeness. Song et al.'s strict all-arm gains show that genuinely multivalued information can matter, yet neither paper pins that phenomenon to an omitted dual vertex or a regime-wide incompleteness statement.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An exact rational counterexample with full active-set rank certification and a regime statement specifying values of n and l for which at least one M-class lies outside the arm-indexed family.

writing_exemplar.why: Its lower-bound construction cleanly separates exhibiting many vertices from characterizing all sharp terms.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: sharp formulas require all dual M-classes -> the multivalued paper constructs a large arm-indexed subfamily -> a lower bound does not establish exhaustiveness -> determine whether genuinely multi-arm vertices lie outside it

method_exemplars.technique: Use M-equivalence, the active-constraint rank criterion, and an exposing-distribution construction to certify a distinct sharp term.
method_exemplars.why_closest: These are the anchor paper's own criteria for distinguishing and exposing sharp dual vertices.

method_exemplars.technique: Use exact response-function optimization to discover a witness, then translate the computational witness into a rational certificate.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The method searches finite response-function models for omitted extremal structures.

method_exemplars.technique: Treat the enlarged vertex family as an intersection-bound index set and adapt binding-set selection and Gaussian approximation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: An omitted vertex is an omitted component in a max/min of estimated linear bounds.

open_problems.open_problem: Can one derive a complete, explicit, nonredundant system of observed-law instrumental inequalities for binary treatment, n-valued outcome, and l>2 instrument, rather than obtaining it by numerical projection or feasibility testing?

source_refs.claim: The paper says observed-law inequalities could be obtained by Fourier-Motzkin elimination, uses computational feasibility instead, and lists explicit instrumental inequalities as future work.
source_refs.author: Yilin Song, F. Richard Guo, K. C. Gary Chan, Thomas S. Richardson

source_refs.claim: For l>2 the paper exhibits a nonredundant exponential family of IV inequalities and a lower bound, but gives a complete sharp characterization only for l=2.
source_refs.author: Arefe Boushehrian, Mohammad Reza Badri, Sina Akbari, Negar Kiyavash

source_refs.claim: Generalized instrumental inequalities are necessary and sufficient for observable violations with binary treatment and outcome and unrestricted instrument, with intersection-bound inference for testing them.
source_refs.author: Desire Kedagni, Ismael Mourifie

open_problems.why_unsolved: Song et al.'s compact joint-counterfactual representation still contains latent probabilities; projecting them out can create a combinatorial explosion. Boushehrian et al. identify many indispensable l>2 rays but do not prove completeness. Necessary-and-sufficient generalized inequalities cover the binary-outcome corner, leaving the n-valued outcome projection and its irredundant extreme-ray structure unresolved.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient irredundant inequality characterization of the observed IV model, proved by an extreme-ray or projection argument and accompanied by an explicit generation algorithm.

writing_exemplar.why: It directly motivates the problem through categorical-IV compatibility, compact latent-space inequalities, falsification, and the deferred projection.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: categorical IV assumptions constrain joint counterfactual laws -> a compact latent-space representation enables optimization -> direct falsification requires eliminating latent probabilities -> obtain explicit observed-law instrumental inequalities

method_exemplars.technique: Combine the finite Strassen coupling characterization with structured projection of coherence-polytope inequalities.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its theorem is the exact latent-space representation whose projection is sought.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt the binary extreme-ray signature calculus and the l>2 rank and nonredundancy witnesses.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Observed-law inequalities correspond to M-distinct extreme rays in the same discrete-IV dual cone.

method_exemplars.technique: Port sample splitting and intersection-bound testing to the resulting explicit, potentially exponential inequality family.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It proves and tests necessary-and-sufficient observed-law IV inequalities in the nearest solved support regime.

open_problems.open_problem: Can the finite explicit max/min representation of multivalued-IV sharp ATE endpoints support uniformly valid, materially less conservative inference that adapts to the unknown set of binding vertices?

source_refs.claim: The paper constructs conservative finite-sample confidence intervals through a KL-divergence confidence region and explicitly calls for less conservative inference.
source_refs.author: Yilin Song, F. Richard Guo, K. C. Gary Chan, Thomas S. Richardson

source_refs.claim: Sharp ATE endpoints are maxima or minima of finitely many linear functionals, but the paper focuses on characterization and computation rather than sampling theory for nonsmooth extrema.
source_refs.author: Arefe Boushehrian, Mohammad Reza Badri, Sina Akbari, Negar Kiyavash

open_problems.why_unsolved: At distributions where several vertex functionals tie or nearly tie, plug-in endpoints are nonsmooth and their limit law depends on an unknown binding set. Song et al.'s KL projection guarantees simultaneous finite-sample coverage but can be conservative, while the smooth delta method is not uniform across changes in the active set; a growing explicit index also requires complexity-aware Gaussian approximation.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniformly valid confidence procedure with an explicit max-of-Gaussian or bootstrap limit theorem, adaptive binding-vertex selection, and coverage guarantees allowing the enumerated index set to grow with n and l.

writing_exemplar.why: It presents categorical-IV bounds, finite-sample inference, empirical use of all arms, and explicitly motivates reducing conservatism.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: categorical-IV bounds are convex-program extrema -> multinomial sampling requires uncertainty propagation -> KL projection gives guaranteed but conservative intervals -> exploit an explicit finite vertex index for adaptive sharp inference

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt intersection-bound bias correction, strong Gaussian approximation, and adaptive inequality selection.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The ATE endpoints are finite intersection bounds indexed by dual vertices.

method_exemplars.technique: Use multiplier bootstrap critical values for maxima, moment selection, and uniform approximation error bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The method permits exponentially many correlated inequalities and proves uniform validity for max statistics.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt calibrated projection so coverage targets the scalar ATE endpoint rather than the full latent parameter region.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is designed to reduce conservatism when projecting a confidence set for a partially identified nuisance parameter.

open_problems.open_problem: What necessary-and-sufficient condition determines when retaining all l>2 instrument arms strictly tightens the sharp ATE interval relative to every two-arm sub-instrument?

source_refs.claim: The paper proves that using all arms gives plug-in identification intervals no wider than deleting an arm and notes that the improvement is sometimes strict, but does not characterize strictness.
source_refs.author: Yilin Song, F. Richard Guo, K. C. Gary Chan, Thomas S. Richardson

source_refs.claim: The paper derives sharp causal-effect bounds for three-arm trials with noncompliance under principal-stratum assumptions, but not the assumption-free ATE strict-gain frontier.
source_refs.author: Jing Cheng, Dylan S. Small
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B

source_refs.claim: The paper constructs multivalued lower-bound families of sharp terms but does not characterize which observed laws expose a term unavailable from every two-arm restriction.
source_refs.author: Arefe Boushehrian, Mohammad Reza Badri, Sina Akbari, Negar Kiyavash

open_problems.why_unsolved: Set inclusion proves only weak tightening when arms are retained. Strictness requires linking the observed law to a supporting dual vertex whose nonconstant coefficients genuinely involve at least three arm blocks; neither the categorical primal characterization nor the known arm-indexed dual subfamily provides an if-and-only-if exposure criterion.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient primal or dual criterion for strict all-arm tightening, plus an exact witness statement identifying an observed-law region where a three-arm-essential vertex uniquely determines an endpoint.

writing_exemplar.why: Its motivating example contrasts all-arm analysis with arm deletion and states the weak-versus-strict tightening phenomenon directly.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: multivalued instruments are often collapsed or an arm is discarded -> retaining all arms cannot widen sharp bounds -> some laws yield strict gains -> characterize exactly when all-arm information is indispensable

method_exemplars.technique: Use the Strassen and coherence-polytope characterization to compare the full-arm feasible projection with every two-arm projection.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Strict tightening is a support-function separation between precisely these compatible counterfactual polytopes.

method_exemplars.technique: Use exposed-vertex arguments and arm-block invariance modulo M-equivalence to certify that an endpoint functional cannot descend from a two-arm subproblem.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The criterion is naturally expressed as exposure of a genuinely multi-arm sharp dual vertex.

method_exemplars.technique: Construct inference for the difference between full-arm and best-subinstrument intersection bounds using joint Gaussian approximation and contact-set selection.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Empirical strict gain compares correlated maxima or minima whose contact sets may change locally.

literature_map:
1. Balke-Pearl (1997, JASA, DOI 10.1080/01621459.1997.10474074) is the foundational binary-IV sharp-ATE formula from latent response types and linear programming.
2. Richardson-Robins (2014, Statistical Science, arXiv:1410.0470) gives an alternative sharp ACE-bound formulation and anchors the quotient-dual perspective.
3. Cheng-Small (2006, JRSSB, DOI 10.1111/j.1467-9868.2006.00568.x) treats three-arm noncompliance bounds under principal-stratum restrictions, not the unrestricted multivalued-IV calculus.
4. Bonet (2001, UAI; arXiv:1301.2258) studies discrete instrumentality tests and the polyhedral structure of observed-law compatibility.
5. Kedagni-Mourifie (2020, Biometrika, DOI 10.1093/biomet/asaa003) gives necessary-and-sufficient generalized IV inequalities in the binary-treatment/binary-outcome corner and intersection-bound testing.
6. Song-Guo-Chan-Richardson (2024, arXiv:2405.09510) gives a categorical-IV latent-space representation but defers explicit observed-law projection and less conservative inference.
7. Song et al. also show that retaining all arms cannot widen plug-in bounds and can strictly tighten them, without an if-and-only-if strictness criterion.
8. Boushehrian-Badri-Akbari-Kiyavash (2026, arXiv:2604.12802) proves exponential lower bounds, a complete l=2 vertex/signature calculus, and only an l>2 subfamily; it leaves multivalued characterization open.
9. Duarte-Finkelstein-Knox-Mummolo-Shpitser (2024, JASA, arXiv:2109.13471) provides automated response-function optimization useful for discovering exact omitted-vertex witnesses.
10. Khachiyan-Boros-Borys-Elbassioni-Gurvich (2008, DCG, DOI 10.1007/s00454-008-9050-5) proves hardness for vertex generation of general unbounded H-polyhedra.
11. Avis-Fukuda (1996, Discrete Applied Mathematics, DOI 10.1016/0166-218X(95)00026-N) supplies reverse search, the closest generic architecture for a recognition-driven polynomial-delay enumerator.
12. Reimers-Stougie (2014, arXiv:1404.5584) gives total-polynomial vertex enumeration under bounded matroid branch-width, suggesting a special matrix decomposition route.
13. Chernozhukov-Lee-Rosen (2013, Econometrica, arXiv:0907.3503) gives bias-corrected, contact-set-adaptive inference for maxima and minima of estimated bounds.
14. Chernozhukov-Chetverikov-Kato (2019, ReStud, arXiv:1312.7614) establishes bootstrap inference for maxima over exponentially many correlated moments.
15. Imbens-Manski (2004, Econometrica, DOI 10.1111/j.1468-0262.2004.00555.x) and Kaido-Molinari-Stoye (2019, Econometrica, arXiv:1601.00934) frame uniform scalar-target coverage and calibrated projection.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1; its bank README leaves an if-and-only-if pairwise-pasting criterion and endpoint inference open.
Inspected its reviewer JSONs through angle0_v3; the strongest suggestion requests a rational 010/101 incompatibility certificate.
Mined pid_iv_bounded_defier_envelope_v1, including the rejected facet-catalogue angle and bank README; exhaustive exposed-basis enumeration and constructive boundary maps remain unproved.
Its angle1_v1 reviewer prescribes covariate-assisted two-cap IV bounds with active-facet inference.
Mined all pid_mte_multi_iv_v1 proposal angles and reviewer rounds; angle2_v3 identifies faulty zero-budget algebra and an optimizer-dependent perturbation set.
Inspected pid_continuous_iv_no_monotonicity_v1 and its bank README; sharp LATE endpoints, finite-mesh convergence, and inference remain open.
No inspected prior proposal directly solves activity-set recognition or polynomial-delay enumeration for the unrestricted l>2 discrete-IV ATE dual.
